mentions.note: Semiconductor cohort now in charge as AI infrastructure powers shift; Nvidia's competitor benefiting from semis-over-software dynamic.
Semiconductor manufacturer in AI chips layer. U.S. manufacturing revival opportunity, competing for data center AI processor designs.

CPU market growth revised to 35% YoY (vs. 18% Nov forecast). Stock jumped 18% on earnings beat. Analyst downgrade one day before earnings proved spectacularly wrong on foundry constraints and upside limits.
Fantastic earnings tonight; fantastic job. CEO Lisa Su on tomorrow. CPUs now gold as data center demand explodes. Short on supply.
CPU shortage replaced glut; analyst downgrades premature—estimates still too low; use dips to buy; galloping continues until growth multiples reached.
Best upside surprise bet for the week; Lisa Su is the best; buying ahead of quarter.
Parabolic move in AI/compute space; Cramer wishes trust owned it; benefits from Fourth Industrial Revolution shortages.
CPU complex beneficiary; up 70% since March 30; part of semiconductor rally but fundamentals solid for data center demand.
CPU for agentic AI replacing Nvidia monolith; both Intel and AMD needed but Cramer missed.
CPU producer (number two after Intel) up 42% since March 30; CPU bottleneck for AI data center is key catalyst alongside GPU demand.
Soared on CPU demand for AI; critical compute partner for hyperscalers; beneficiary of Nvidia ecosystem.
CPU short supply signaled by Taiwan Semi; stocks up already but better than speculative compute plays; real chip producer.
Taiwan Semi confirmed CPUs in short supply; buy AMD and Intel like everyone else instead of speculative alternates.
Hardware semiconductor player gaining momentum; benefiting from hardware vs. software rotation and AI demand.
Semiconductor sector under pressure from export restriction rumors; would soar if Trump administration clarifies it's not restricting overseas sales of best chips.
Down 3.5% on same program trade as Nvidia competitors despite no new news; indiscriminate hardware sector rotation, not AMD-specific concerns.
Down 3.5% today along with broader hardware rout; caught in program trade despite competitive dynamics; hold existing positions.
Fear terrific numbers will still result in stock decline — new pattern for semis; reports after close.
Rallied nicely as Intel stumbled; better factored CPU shortage into guidance. Regret not holding for Trust.
Competing in data center chip market; benefits from OpenAI buildout but exposed to same OpenAI risk as NVIDIA.
Beneficiary of TSMC's strong AI chip orders and hyperscaler demand confirmation; as Taiwan Semi's client, AMD should share in strength.
AI hardware rally led by semiconductors; AMD participating in infrastructure surge.
Fantastic chip company; not as favored as Nvidia but equally valid buy at $500 price point; 80B buyback + growth profile supportive.
